message:
fix(io_internal): _IO_adjust_column glibc-parity rewrite (bd-ryp2g)

`_IO_adjust_column` and `_IO_adjust_wcolumn` were tracking columns
forward through every byte/wchar with tab-to-8-column expansion and
CR-zeroing. Glibc's libio/genops.c implementation is completely
different: scan BACKWARD from the end of the buffer for the last
'\n', and either return `count - last_newline_index - 1` or, if no
newline is present, `start + count`. Tabs and CR are not special-
cased.

The divergence breaks ABI parity for any third-party library that
depends on the documented glibc semantics of these symbols
(pretty-printers, indent trackers, libstdc++ stream column
counters). It is also a small correctness footgun: the old impl
panicked in debug for column counters near `u32::MAX` due to
unchecked `c + 1` / `c + 8` arithmetic, and could return a negative
column counter to the caller after `c as c_int`.

Rewrite both functions to mirror the glibc algorithm. Membrane
policy gates are preserved exactly. Sums saturated to `c_int::MAX`
to keep the per-stream column counter monotonic without overflow.

Add 6 regression tests pinning glibc semantics:
- no-newline path returns start + count
- with-newline path returns distance from last newline (start ignored)
- '\r' and '\t' are NOT special-cased
- null/zero/negative count returns start unchanged
- wide-char variant matches the same semantics for wchar_t '\n' (0x0A)
- wide-char no-newline path

Verified: 52/52 io_internal_native_file_test pass; clippy clean.
